## Reviews

### Review by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) (commented) — 2024-08-19T13:32:09Z

/lgtm
/approve

### Review by [@tenzen-y](https://github.com/tenzen-y) (commented) — 2024-08-19T13:37:54Z

- **Makefile:176** by [@tenzen-y](https://github.com/tenzen-y) — 2024-08-19T13:37:50Z
```diff
@@ -169,9 +169,11 @@ lint-fix: golangci-lint
 shell-lint: ## Run shell linting.
 	$(PROJECT_DIR)/hack/shellcheck/verify.sh
 
+PATHS_TO_VERIFY := config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
 .PHONY: verify
 verify: gomod-verify ci-lint fmt-verify shell-lint toc-verify manifests generate update-helm generate-apiref generate-kueuectl-docs prepare-release-branch
-	git --no-pager diff --exit-code config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
+	git --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
+	if git ls-files --exclude-standard --others $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY) | grep -q . ; then exit 1; fi
```

  ```suggestion
  	git --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
  	git --cashed --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
  ```
  
  Instead of `ls-files` command, couldn't we check just staged files like this, right?

### Review by [@mbobrovskyi](https://github.com/mbobrovskyi) (commented) — 2024-08-19T14:20:46Z

- **Makefile:176** by [@mbobrovskyi](https://github.com/mbobrovskyi) — 2024-08-19T14:20:46Z
```diff
@@ -169,9 +169,11 @@ lint-fix: golangci-lint
 shell-lint: ## Run shell linting.
 	$(PROJECT_DIR)/hack/shellcheck/verify.sh
 
+PATHS_TO_VERIFY := config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
 .PHONY: verify
 verify: gomod-verify ci-lint fmt-verify shell-lint toc-verify manifests generate update-helm generate-apiref generate-kueuectl-docs prepare-release-branch
-	git --no-pager diff --exit-code config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
+	git --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
+	if git ls-files --exclude-standard --others $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY) | grep -q . ; then exit 1; fi
```

  `--cached` flag doesn't allow to check not added to git files.

### Review by [@tenzen-y](https://github.com/tenzen-y) (commented) — 2024-08-19T14:26:45Z

- **Makefile:176** by [@tenzen-y](https://github.com/tenzen-y) — 2024-08-19T14:26:45Z
```diff
@@ -169,9 +169,11 @@ lint-fix: golangci-lint
 shell-lint: ## Run shell linting.
 	$(PROJECT_DIR)/hack/shellcheck/verify.sh
 
+PATHS_TO_VERIFY := config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
 .PHONY: verify
 verify: gomod-verify ci-lint fmt-verify shell-lint toc-verify manifests generate update-helm generate-apiref generate-kueuectl-docs prepare-release-branch
-	git --no-pager diff --exit-code config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
+	git --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
+	if git ls-files --exclude-standard --others $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY) | grep -q . ; then exit 1; fi
```

  That's right. So, I added 2 commands, `git diif` and `git diff --cached` as you can see above.

### Review by [@mbobrovskyi](https://github.com/mbobrovskyi) (commented) — 2024-08-19T15:12:56Z

- **Makefile:176** by [@mbobrovskyi](https://github.com/mbobrovskyi) — 2024-08-19T15:12:56Z
```diff
@@ -169,9 +169,11 @@ lint-fix: golangci-lint
 shell-lint: ## Run shell linting.
 	$(PROJECT_DIR)/hack/shellcheck/verify.sh
 
+PATHS_TO_VERIFY := config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
 .PHONY: verify
 verify: gomod-verify ci-lint fmt-verify shell-lint toc-verify manifests generate update-helm generate-apiref generate-kueuectl-docs prepare-release-branch
-	git --no-pager diff --exit-code config/components apis charts/kueue/templates client-go site/
+	git --no-pager diff --exit-code $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY)
+	if git ls-files --exclude-standard --others $(PATHS_TO_VERIFY) | grep -q . ; then exit 1; fi
```

  To be sure I didn't got how it can help.
  
  > git diff: It shows only those changes of tracked files which are present in working directory.
  > git diff --cached: It shows only those changes of tracked files which are present in staging area.
  
  But we are looking solution to show untracked files.

  Alright, let's revisit here once we face the same error.
